title: Phyllorkis tenuicaulis
taxonomy: Phyllorkis tenuicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to parts of the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This species is classified as an epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants for support. Phyllorkis tenuicaulis is a terrestrial orchid that is often found in shaded and humid environments. The classification of Phyllorkis tenuicaulis as a species within the family Orchidaceae places it in the order Asparagales.

intro: Phyllorkis tenuicaulis, also known as Phyllorkis, is a plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae) and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis tenuicaulis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
